name: add-agent-response-messages-20260401-0221
idea: Add agent response messages to the effect generation flow. Three things: (1) When the ai-generate-effect edge function completes (success or retry-success), the response should include an optional 'message' field — a user-facing message from the LLM explaining what it built, any creative choices it made, or acknowledging the user's request. The LLM prompt should ask for this. (2) The user's effect creation prompt input should also support questions/conversation — if the user asks a question instead of requesting an effect, the edge function should detect this and return a conversational response (message only, no code) instead of generating code. This means the edge function needs a triage step. (3) On the frontend (EffectCreatorPanel), show the agent's message in an ephemeral toast/box near the effect creator. Since the flow is synchronous (user waits for response), show the message when the response arrives and let it persist until dismissed or the user starts a new generation. Think about: where in the UI this message appears, how it interacts with the existing effect preview, whether it should be a toast vs inline message vs expandable panel, and how question-only responses (no code) should be displayed differently from effect-generated responses.

overrides.note: User feedback: (1) Question detection should work in both create and edit mode. (2) Use a distinct QUESTION_RESPONSE marker separate from MESSAGE. (3) Restructure the system prompt to have a clear triage instruction at the top level, not buried in output rules. (4) In edit mode with a question, preserve existing code and just return a conversational answer.

notes.note: User feedback: (1) Question detection should work in both create and edit mode. (2) Use a distinct QUESTION_RESPONSE marker separate from MESSAGE. (3) Restructure the system prompt to have a clear triage instruction at the top level, not buried in output rules. (4) In edit mode with a question, preserve existing code and just return a conversational answer.

last_gate.rationale: Both blocking flags identify real correctness risks that the current plan doesn't adequately address. Flag 1: The plan adds a small conversational exception to OUTPUT_RULES but leaves the rest of the prompt (system message, user message templates) strongly framing every request as code generation. An LLM receiving "Build a ... Remotion effect" in the user message with a small exception clause buried in output rules will unreliably detect questions. The plan needs to restructure the prompt more carefully — likely by moving question detection to the system message level and conditionally changing the user message template when no existing code is present. Flag 2: The `no exports.default + MESSAGE => question` heuristic is genuinely unsafe. A malformed code generation (LLM includes MESSAGE but forgets the default export) would silently succeed as a "question response" instead of triggering the retry path. The plan needs a more robust discriminator — e.g., a distinct `// QUESTION_RESPONSE: true` marker that the LLM only outputs for actual questions, separate from `// MESSAGE:` which appears on all responses. This way, malformed code with a MESSAGE line but no QUESTION_RESPONSE marker still hits validation and gets retried.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Iteration 1 with a weighted score of 4.0. Two significant flags remain open, both in the correctness category, targeting the core triage/parsing logic that is central to the plan's design. No flags have been resolved yet and no prior iterations exist. Preflight checks all pass. The flags are well-evidenced against specific code locations and represent likely runtime failures, not theoretical concerns.
warnings: The prompt restructuring for question detection should be tested against edit-mode prompts specifically — edit mode has even stronger code-generation framing than create mode.
Consider whether question detection should be disabled entirely in edit mode (where code already exists) to avoid the complexity of supporting it there in v1.

settled_decisions.decision: Single LLM call (no separate triage classifier) is the right latency tradeoff — but the prompt must clearly partition question vs code responses at the system message level, not as a footnote in output rules.
settled_decisions.rationale: A separate triage call adds 2-5s latency for every request. The single-call approach is fine if the prompt is structured to make question detection reliable.

settled_decisions.decision: Inline message box (not toast) is the correct UI pattern for agent messages.
settled_decisions.rationale: Toasts auto-dismiss and are disconnected from the interaction context. An inline dismissible box near the prompt area keeps the message in context and lets the user reference it while composing their next prompt.

settled_decisions.decision: MESSAGE metadata line on code responses is the right approach for agent commentary — just needs to be decoupled from question detection.
settled_decisions.rationale: Adding a // MESSAGE: line to successful code generations is low-risk and useful. The issue is only when MESSAGE is used as part of the question-detection heuristic.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: ITERATE. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
